message:
test(ft-conformance): linalg.pinv numpy subprocess conformance [bd-c36b]

Adds torch_linalg_pinv_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering 7
matrices: square nonsingular 3x3, identity 4x4, scaled identity 3x3,
diagonal 4x4, tall 4x2 full column rank, wide 2x4 full row rank,
1x1 scalar.

Per-element relative tolerance of 1e-8. The Moore-Penrose
pseudoinverse is unique (no sign ambiguity), so element-wise
comparison is meaningful — unlike Q/U/V which require structural
invariant checks. Both numpy and FrankenTorch build A+ via SVD:
A+ = V @ diag(1/S) @ U^T, so the error envelope is double the SVD
envelope (1e-9) due to the (A^T A) conditioning component.

Rank-deficient cases are intentionally omitted because they
exercise the basis-completion path tracked under frankentorch-zs8a.
Re-enable after the SVD bug is fixed.

Sister harness to the svd / inv / solve / qr / cholesky / det /
slogdet harnesses. Files closure for the pinverse slice of
frankentorch-c36b.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
